meta.source: TradingView watchlist + FRED live scrape (Chrome extension)

inflation.label: Cooling
decomp: Oil -0.69% today, -4% cumulative from surge high

confidence_meta.reason: No binary catalyst within 48h (CPI Jul 14 is 4 days out, FOMC Jul 28-29 is 18 days out); MOVE (68.89) below 80 threshold; all four scores improving in the same direction, not split 2-2

structured_conclusion.likely_rotation: Semis/enabling-layer chain consolidating after yesterday's 5-12% breakout; credit-sensitive financials (ALLY/CACC/COF) and vol-sensitive names continuing to participate
structured_conclusion.korea_gate: 1/3 — USDKRW pass only (EWY below SMA20, KOSPI below 8,200)

bias: Financial conditions continue to ease from restrictive levels — TGA drew down $106B this week (the largest weekly liquidity add in recent runs), reserves crossed back above $3T, and MOVE finally broke its multi-day climb (-4.86%). Nominal yields backed up modestly (2-5bps) across the curve alongside stable breakevens, which reads as growth optimism rather than real-rate panic. Absolute yield levels remain historically high (10Y 4.57%, 2Y 4.21%), so this is 'less restrictive,' not 'accommodative.'

supporting: TGA -$106B/wk, reserves back above $3T for the first time in recent runs
MOVE -4.86%, finally confirming equity-vol calm after several sessions of climbing
USDJPY back below 162 BOJ threshold (161.72 vs 162.29 last run)
Oil extending its pullback, -0.69% today (~-4% cumulative from the recent surge high)
VIX -3.72% to 15.26; credit-sensitive financials (ALLY/CACC/COF) all up again today
against: Semis/enabling-layer chain pulling back today (-1% to -2.5%) after yesterday's outsized 5-12% breakout — one-day digestion, not yet confirmed as healthy
Nominal yields backed up 2-5bps across the curve even as liquidity metrics improved
HY OAS widened modestly (+3bps) even as credit-sensitive equities rallied
RRP buffer nearly exhausted (~$0.5B) — the next TGA rebuild will pull directly from reserves with no cushion left
Korea gate remains closed 1/3 — EWY ~13pts below SMA20, KOSPI ~725pts below the 8,200 trigger
counterargs.read: The plumbing data confirms the liquidity-easing thesis with real force — this week's TGA drawdown and the return of reserves above $3T are the most unambiguous liquidity-positive prints in recent runs, and MOVE's break lower removes the last major 'against' signal from the prior update. The one wrinkle is the semis chain's pullback today after an outsized rally, which reads as digestion rather than reversal given the broader vol and credit backdrop are both calming and breakevens held stable through the yield backup. The bigger structural caveat is the RRP buffer: with it near zero, the Fed's plumbing has less shock absorption left, so the next TGA rebuild around month-end could pressure reserves directly. Korea gate remains the cleanest 'not yet' signal — still 1/3, unchanged.

trade_table.rationale: Pulling back -1% to -2.5% today after yesterday's 5-12% breakout; broader liquidity/vol backdrop still supportive but wait for stabilization before adding size.
10Y ticked up ~3bps run-over-run despite the liquidity add; TLT -0.13% today. Absolute yields remain restrictive.
All three up again today; HY OAS only modestly wider (+3bps); MOVE breaking lower removes a key credit-vol headwind.
Gate still closed 1/3 — EWY ~13pts below SMA20, KOSPI ~725pts below the 8,200 trigger despite today's +2.5% KOSPI move.
Back below 162 BOJ threshold (161.72); intervention risk reduced but the US-Japan 10Y spread widened to ~186bps, so a reversal could resnap the pair higher.

catalysts.note: Key input for the disinflation thesis given oil's recent volatility

duration_score.classification: Liquidity-Driven Reflation / Growth Optimism (bonds lag, risk-on leads)
duration_score.reason: TGA draw-down and the reserves rebuild above $3T are unambiguously liquidity-positive, but nominal yields backed up 2-5bps across the curve alongside stable breakevens and calm credit — consistent with a growth-optimism/reflation-lite tape rather than a Fed-easing rally in duration specifically. MOVE breaking lower (-4.86%) confirms vol compression despite the yield backup.

james_framework.what_market_IS_saying: Liquidity plumbing is adding support while nominal yields back up modestly on growth optimism, not real-rate panic — breakevens held stable rather than collapsing.
james_framework.pain_trade: Not the pain trade today. Stable breakevens plus calm credit alongside the yield backup argue against a real-rate-driven tightening narrative.
james_framework.watch_signal: If breakevens start collapsing while nominal yields stay elevated → transitions to the real-rate pain trade. Watch T5YIE/T10YIE alongside DFII5 into CPI (Jul 14).
